original_hypothesis.title: Manager can become irrecoverably bricked (set to address(0) or non-callable contract), freezing all epoch progression and user cancellations
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The vault’s core progression functions (fulfillDeposits/fulfillWithdrawals/cancelDeposit/cancelWithdrawal/manage) are onlyManager. setManager has no validation against address(0) or against contracts that cannot operate. If owner misconfigures manager, the vault cannot progress epochs or cancel requests, trapping deposits and pending withdrawals indefinitely.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a common operational failure mode: a single wrong admin transaction can permanently lock funds because users have no self-cancel path (INV-ACCESS-002).
deployment_assumptions: Owner sets manager to address(0) or to an address without a usable signing capability / non-functional contract
No alternative access path exists for epoch management
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: No deployment scripts provided; cannot verify manager initialization/rotation safeguards.
affected_functions: manage(address[] calldata targets, bytes[] calldata data, uint256[] calldata values)

inventory_refs.function: manage(address[] calldata targets, bytes[] calldata data, uint256[] calldata values)

proof_of_signal.pattern: setManager() lacks nonzero/contract validation; onlyManager gates all operational functions

preconditions: Owner makes an incorrect setManager call
There are queued deposits/withdrawals requiring manager action
Users cannot self-cancel and no emergency function exists

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Single point of failure on manager address with no guardrails

operational_questions: Are there off-chain procedures preventing setting manager to invalid addresses?
Is there a deployment-time assertion for nonzero manager and post-rotation tests?
Is manager intended to be a contract (automation) or EOA?
validation_requirements: Review operational scripts and transaction policies for manager rotations
Consider adding on-chain validation checks (nonzero, optional interface support) in future versions

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Wait for misconfiguration (or induce via governance/ops processes if attacker has influence)
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Capitalize on frozen withdrawals/deposits (secondary market discounts/premiums)
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Sustain by absence of cancellation/fulfillment path

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Total operational halt; funds stuck in queues
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: High (requires owner still available to correct; if also lost, permanent)
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: all onlyManager functions and user access to liquidity

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: manager == address(0) or unexpected manager contract bytecode changes
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: No DepositsFulfilled/WithdrawalsFulfilled events for prolonged periods

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
